name: US Dollar
description: The US Dollar is the world's primary reserve currency, used in global trade and as a benchmark for commodity pricing.
funFact: About 80% of all US dollar bills are $100 notes, mostly held outside the US.

name: Euro
description: The Euro is the official currency of 20 EU member states, making it the second most traded currency globally.
funFact: Euro banknotes feature architectural styles from different European periods, but the buildings shown are fictional.

name: British Pound
description: The British Pound Sterling is the oldest currency still in use, with origins dating back over 1,200 years.
funFact: The pound gets its name from a pound weight of silver in Anglo-Saxon England.

name: Japanese Yen
description: The Japanese Yen is the third most traded currency and a popular safe-haven asset during market uncertainty.
funFact: Japan was the first country to issue banknotes in the 7th century.

name: Swiss Franc
description: The Swiss Franc is known for its stability, backed by Switzerland's strong economy and political neutrality.
funFact: Swiss banknotes are among the most secure in the world, with up to 18 security features.

name: Canadian Dollar
description: The Canadian Dollar is heavily influenced by commodity prices, especially oil and natural gas exports.
funFact: Canadian polymer banknotes can survive a cycle in the washing machine.

name: Australian Dollar
description: The Australian Dollar is the fifth most traded currency, closely tied to commodity exports and Asian trade.
funFact: Australia was the first country to use polymer banknotes nationwide in 1988.

name: New Zealand Dollar
description: The New Zealand Dollar, nicknamed 'Kiwi', is influenced by dairy exports and tourism.
funFact: The NZ $5 note features Sir Edmund Hillary, first to summit Mount Everest.

name: Chinese Yuan
description: The Chinese Yuan (Renminbi) is the currency of the world's second-largest economy, increasingly used in international trade.
funFact: All Chinese banknotes feature Mao Zedong's portrait.

name: Hong Kong Dollar
description: The Hong Kong Dollar operates under a linked exchange rate system, pegged to the US Dollar.
funFact: Three different commercial banks issue HKD banknotes, each with unique designs.

name: Singapore Dollar
description: The Singapore Dollar reflects one of Asia's strongest and most transparent economies.
funFact: Singapore's $10,000 note was once the highest-value banknote in circulation worldwide.

name: Swedish Krona
description: The Swedish Krona is the currency of Sweden, one of the most cashless societies in the world.
funFact: Sweden is moving toward becoming the world's first completely cashless society.

name: Norwegian Krone
description: The Norwegian Krone is backed by one of the world's largest sovereign wealth funds, fueled by oil revenues.
funFact: Norway's Government Pension Fund owns about 1.5% of all listed stocks worldwide.

name: Danish Krone
description: The Danish Krone is pegged to the Euro through the ERM II mechanism.
funFact: Danish banknotes feature bridges of Denmark and prehistoric artifacts.

name: South Korean Won
description: The South Korean Won reflects one of Asia's most dynamic and tech-driven economies.
funFact: South Korea's 50,000 won note features Shin Saimdang, a 16th-century artist.

name: Indian Rupee
description: The Indian Rupee is the currency of the world's most populous country and fifth-largest economy.
funFact: The ₹ symbol was designed by D. Udaya Kumar and adopted in 2010.

name: Brazilian Real
description: The Brazilian Real is the currency of South America's largest economy, influenced by commodity exports.
funFact: Brazil has had eight different currencies since 1942.

name: Mexican Peso
description: The Mexican Peso is the most traded currency in Latin America and among the top 15 globally.
funFact: The '$' symbol originated from the Mexican Peso, not the US Dollar.

name: South African Rand
description: The South African Rand is the most traded African currency, named after the Witwatersrand gold ridge.
funFact: All South African banknotes feature Nelson Mandela's portrait.

name: Turkish Lira
description: The Turkish Lira is the currency of Turkey, a major emerging market bridging Europe and Asia.
funFact: Turkey removed six zeros from the lira in 2005, converting 1,000,000 old lira to 1 new lira.

name: Russian Ruble
description: The Russian Ruble is one of the oldest currencies in the world, dating back to the 13th century.
funFact: The word 'ruble' comes from the Russian verb 'rubit' meaning 'to chop'.

name: Polish Zloty
description: The Polish Zloty is the currency of Poland, Central Europe's largest economy.
funFact: Zloty means 'golden' in Polish, referencing gold coins used in medieval trade.

name: Thai Baht
description: The Thai Baht is the currency of Thailand, a popular tourist destination and growing economy.
funFact: It is illegal to step on Thai currency as it bears the King's image.

name: Indonesian Rupiah
description: The Indonesian Rupiah is the currency of Southeast Asia's largest economy.
funFact: Indonesia's 100,000 rupiah note is worth less than $7 USD.

name: Malaysian Ringgit
description: The Malaysian Ringgit is the currency of Malaysia, a major trading hub in Southeast Asia.
funFact: 'Ringgit' means 'jagged' in Malay, referring to the serrated edges of Spanish silver coins.

name: Philippine Peso
description: The Philippine Peso is the currency of the Philippines, driven by remittances and BPO services.
funFact: The Philippines receives over $30 billion annually in overseas worker remittances.

name: Czech Koruna
description: The Czech Koruna is the currency of the Czech Republic, a strong Central European economy.
funFact: The Czech Republic has one of the lowest unemployment rates in the EU.

name: Israeli Shekel
description: The Israeli New Shekel is the currency of Israel, a global leader in tech startups.
funFact: The shekel is one of the oldest currency names, dating back to ancient Mesopotamia.

name: UAE Dirham
description: The UAE Dirham is pegged to the US Dollar and reflects the UAE's oil wealth and trade hub status.
funFact: Dubai's economy has diversified so much that oil now accounts for less than 1% of its GDP.

name: Saudi Riyal
description: The Saudi Riyal is pegged to the US Dollar and backed by the world's largest oil reserves.
funFact: Saudi Arabia's oil reserves are estimated at over 260 billion barrels.
